meaning: adj.原因的,因果关系的,表示原因或理由的n.表示原因的词(如连词)
examples: Rawlins stresses that it is impossible to prove a causal link between the drug and the deaths.
"Most of these studies don't allow for causal inferences," he said.
Religious and occult predictions, however, boast no causal logic whatsoever.
At which point let me float a causal argument for what is going on here.
Early indications suggest the rookie company founders are spread all across the effectual-to-causal scale.
